reviews.review_description: Love the 3rd person view for a change. Quite a few MMA moves. Very challenging!

I won this game in a comp.

I assumed this'd be 1st-person like all the other fighting games I've played, but it's actually 3rd-person, with you viewing the fighting from the ring.
It does make sense considering the MMA aspect.

I play these types of games for fitness as much as for fun, and this certainly got my heart pumping after a few fights!

The graphics are good, with fighters becoming bloodied and battered after a gruelling round.
Audio is decent, with solid sounding punches. The music is serviceable, not distracting.

The one thing I would change is the button mashing. I'd prefer if there was an alternative to just pressing a button quickly, as it didn't exactly feel very immersive.

Overall though a very promising experience so far. I look forward to the full release.

This is a complete fighting experience, with a detailed tutorial and a career mode. The game offers a wide variety of fighters, extensive customization options and a lot of new fighting techniques. You should definitely try it !

This VR Boxing & MMA game delivers a powerful and immersive combat experience on Meta Quest. The controls feel natural, and the variety of boxing actions—jabs, hooks, uppercuts, blocks, and MMA-style moves—make every fight intense and engaging.
I really liked how you can choose different fighting actions and adapt your strategy against the opponent. The opponent AI feels responsive, which keeps matches challenging and exciting.
One of the best features is the real-time stats system. Being able to see performance stats like punches thrown, accuracy, stamina, and overall fight data adds a competitive and professional feel to the game. It also helps track improvement over time.
The gameplay is smooth, physically engaging, and gives a great workout while still being fun. Whether you’re a boxing fan or just want an action-packed VR experience, this game is definitely worth playing

reviews.review_title: A crappy game with a bad control system
reviews.review_description:
The control system doesn't make use of VR.
At the very least,Arm movements should be synchronized with the player.
This is just a 2D game with 3D vision, not VR.
The relationship between the stick and the direction of movement is also unnatural.
If the control system were completely redesigned, it would be the best game ever.

I received the key from DC GA.
At first, I thought of a first-person boxing simulation like Thrill of Fight in the title of boxing, but it was surprising that I had to control a player from a third-person perspective. Although in third-person-view, I have to swing my arms and hands myself, and I can try various body techniques of MMA by combining various keys and actions, so it is much immersive.
I think it will also be useful for studying MMA skills because you can defeat strong opponents by using various techniques of MMA, not just punches.
I didn't enjoy MMA much, but I think it will be more fun to watch MMA in the future as I get used to it by doing various techniques by myself.
bad game

Game won thanks to Giveaway and Video. Beautiful graphics. Good playability. A period of learning is necessary to master the different moves. Too bad the game is not translated into French. The tutorial is useful but the different actions needed to make the moves should be better explained. The fight with a view outside the ring is nice. For total immersion the view in place of the fighter would be an undeniable plus. Congratulations to the developers for this realistic, fun arcade-style fighting game. Excellent value for money.

This is a well-designed VR experience. After a brief adjustment period to understand the controller mechanics and movement system, the gameplay becomes intuitive and engaging. Once familiar with the interactions, the overall experience is smooth, immersive, and enjoyable.

Intense, immersive, and incredibly fun. The boxing and MMA mechanics feel realistic, tracking is solid, and every fight is a proper workout. One of the best VR combat games on Meta Horizon. Highly recommended.
Fun, straightforward game. Great for a quick daily workout and de-stressing. Can’t wait for more updates to see how the game evolves!

This game is a nice change to the usual first person vr fighting games. Instead you are in third person physically doing the punching actions but seeing your character grapple, punch and kick the opponent. It's unique and is very fun. The game offers a tutorial which I am grateful because jumping right without learning how to play is eye opening for sure. The game is in early access so there are definitely areas to improve on such as character control and more features but it has a great baseline to building upon.
its not even 1st person and the movements I do dont work. Also all of the names are spelt incorrectly. For example, CONOR MCGREGOR is spelt KONER MCGREGER. Like wth?
Bought this game 20 minutes ago and would’ve rather had lit my $6.99 on fire. Absolutely terrible game.
Realistic, accurate, and interesting.

VR Boxing and MMA delivers an engaging mix of traditional controller inputs and immersive VR motion combat. Half of the gameplay relies on precise stick control, while the other half lets you throw real punches to dominate your opponent. The unique side-of-the-ring perspective gives a great sense of action and strategy, perfect for reading attacks and planning your counters.

The training mode is especially rewarding — unlocking new fighters, skills, and abilities across all major MMA disciplines. Progress feels natural as you build power, stamina, and even manage energy boosts.

While multiplayer isn’t available yet, the game’s solid foundation and fun mechanics make it an easy recommendation for MMA fans. Here’s hoping the studio adds online matches soon to complete the experience!

Really smooth and immersive gameplay with controls that feel natural right from the start. The fights stay exciting thanks to responsive AI and a good mix of moves. It actually feels like you're in a real match, and on top of that, it’s a great workout. Definitely worth playing if you enjoy action-packed VR games.

I enjoyed it a lot! The game gives a really immersive fighting experience where you can throw punches like jabs, hooks, and uppercuts using both hands, which feels very satisfying in VR.
One thing I really liked is how physical the gameplay feels. You actually move, dodge, and fight, so it’s not just a game—it’s also a great workout.

reviews.review_title: Super Fun & Intense VR Fighting Game
reviews.review_description:
Had a really good time playing this VR boxing/MMA game on Quest. It feels very immersive, and the controls are easy to pick up but still give you enough depth to enjoy different fighting styles. You’ve got all the classic punches like jabs, hooks, uppercuts, plus blocks and some MMA-style moves, which keeps things exciting.
What I enjoyed the most is how you can adjust your approach depending on the opponent. The AI reacts well, so fights don’t feel repetitive and actually keep you on your toes.
The live stats feature is a nice touch too. Seeing things like punch count, accuracy, stamina, etc., makes it feel more competitive and helps you notice your progress over time.
Overall, the game runs smoothly, is pretty engaging, and even works as a decent workout. If you're into combat games or just want something active in VR, this is definitely worth checking out.

I thought it was 1st person worst controls ever not even good settings all ain't different all it says was turn off music that's it worst game ever not worth paying for should be free I rather whatch paint dry 😡😡😡😡
It is ok but I thought it would be you fighting not your character  and you can’t refund either so it’s crap sorry

Just got this boxing game.
Jump immediately into the tutorial,  but didn’t know what to do. It took a while before I got the moves.
N the tutorial you have to do the moves that are explained. As soon as those are done, and the red text change to green, you got the move.
Tutorial is very good explained. 
I suggest to do the tutorial before get into the real fight.
Avoid the attacks of the opposite, and tackle him to go for victory.

Only thing that I miss is a female character. 
But after all, a good game. A good workout.
Really fun boxing games and they're good exercise 👍

in this game you are in 3 rd person crasy.
 do not reccomend

VR Boxing & MMA packs everything you’d want from a true combat experience in VR — powerful strikes, smooth dodging, and intense one-on-one action. Whether you’re boxing, clinching, or going for the takedown, the controls feel tight and responsive with great low-latency feedback that keeps every punch and block satisfying.

The game modes offer real variety: Quick Clash for instant action, Road to Glory to build your fighter’s legacy, and Elite Showdown for true tests of skill. You can recruit coaches, train hard, and customize your fighter across multiple weight classes. The grind is rewarding as you unlock new moves, gear, and upgrades that shape your own fighting style.

A solid, high-energy MMA experience that feels authentic, challenging, and a blast to master — a must-try for combat sports fans in VR!

VR Boxing & MMA puts you directly in the spotlight, gloved and determined.
The game captures the tension of the fight: the punches are visual, the dodges responsive, and you feel the physical exertion. The studio aimed for simplicity and immediate impact.
The third-person view offers a more arcade-oriented gameplay than an immersive one.
Technically, the atmosphere is realistic and functional: the ring, the audience, the crowd noise, the impact effects, and the animations are well done.
Pros: Varied fighting styles, fighter customization and upgrades, 3 game modes!
Cons: Some moments still suffer from a lack of detail (textures, sometimes basic AI), and the gameplay depth is currently limited, but it's in early access.
Conclusion: The essentials are there: moving, blocking, punching, feeling the impact.
This title checks many boxes. And if it receives the updates it deserves, it could become a must-have in the "VR fighting" genre.

Its a out of body perspective boxing game 
Never saw something like this, it was a nice experience
Keep ur good job up dev ❤️

I stepped into the ring and felt every blow thanks to total immersion in virtual reality!
Thanks to the Giveaways & Videos group on Facebook for the free game key!
Absolutely loved it—super immersive, responsive controls, and really makes you feel like you’re in the ring
Good controls, levels overall, great experience.
